steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the trigonometric ratio from the right triangle
steps.content: In the right triangle, $\theta$ is at the base with the height (28.4 inches) opposite to it and the horizontal distance $x$ adjacent to it. Using the tangent ratio: $$\tan \theta = \frac{28.4}{x}$$

steps.title: Apply the legal constraint
steps.content: The law requires $\tan \theta \leq \frac{1}{12}$. Substituting the expression from step 1: $$\frac{28.4}{x} \leq \frac{1}{12}$$ The least possible value of $x$ occurs when the inequality becomes an equality (the ramp angle is at its maximum legal steepness).
